title: Fix Dashboard page failing to load

requirements.summary: Fix the Dashboard page (/dashboard) to load correctly by handling undefined/null/error states from useGetStats, adding proper loading spinners and error boundaries so the page never crashes
acceptanceCriteria: Navigating to /dashboard no longer results in a blank page or crash in the deployed version
The Dashboard renders a loading state while stats data is being fetched
If the backend getStats call fails, the Dashboard shows a user-friendly error message instead of crashing
If stats data is undefined or empty, the Dashboard still renders gracefully with zero-state messaging
All stat cards, category breakdown, top posts lists, and year distribution sections render correctly once data is available

file_operations.description: Add comprehensive error handling and loading states for useGetStats data. Wrap all stat rendering logic in conditional checks to handle undefined, null, or empty data gracefully. Add a loading spinner component that displays while isLoading is true. Add an error boundary or error state UI that displays a user-friendly message when isError is true or when the query fails. Ensure all references to stats.totalPosts, stats.categoryCounts, stats.topUpvotedPosts, and stats.topConnectedPosts are guarded with optional chaining or null checks. Add zero-state messaging for empty arrays or undefined stats. Ensure the page structure always renders (no conditional return of entire component) so navigation never results in a blank page.

requirements.summary: Audit and fix the useGetStats hook to handle actor initialization delay, return a stable React Query structure, and prevent unhandled promise rejections when actor is undefined
acceptanceCriteria: useGetStats does not throw when actor is undefined or not yet initialized
useGetStats correctly calls getStats on the backend actor once it is available
The hook returns { data, isLoading, isError } in a shape usable by Dashboard.tsx
The query is enabled only when the actor is available (enabled: !!actor)

file_operations.description: Fix the useGetStats hook to guard against undefined actor by adding an enabled option (enabled: !!actor) to the useQuery configuration. Wrap the queryFn in a check that returns a promise rejection or throws a controlled error if actor is undefined, preventing unhandled promise rejections. Ensure the queryFn only calls actor.getStats() when actor is truthy. Verify the hook returns a stable { data, isLoading, isError, error } structure compatible with React Query and usable by Dashboard.tsx. Add type annotations to ensure PostStats return type matches the backend interface.
